dust 0.8.3

Iterate a dust model

Source: R/iterate.R
dust_iterate.Rd

Iterate a dust model over time. This is a wrapper around calling $run() and $state() repeatedly without doing anything very interesting with the output. It is provided mostly as a quick-and-dirty way of getting started with a model. This function is deprecated since v0.7.8 and will be removed in a future version of dust. Please use the $simulate() method directly on a dust object - see below for details to update your code.

dust_iterate(model, steps, index = NULL)

Arguments

model

A model, compiled with dust::dust() and initialised
steps

A vector of steps - the first step must be same as the model's step (i.e., $step())
index

An optional index to filter the results with.

Value

A 3d array of model outputs. The first dimension is model state, the second is particle number and the third is time, so that output[i, j, k] is the ith variable, jth particle and kth step.

Details

To migrate to use the method, set the index into the model (if using), and use the simulate method:

mod$set_index(index) # if using
mod$simulate(steps)

Examples

# Same random walk example as in ?dust
model <- dust::dust_example("walk")

# Create a model with 100 particles, starting at step 0
obj <- model$new(list(sd = 1), 0, 100)

# Steps that we want to report at:
steps <- seq(0, 400, by = 4)

# Run the simulation:
res <- obj$simulate(steps)

# Output is 1 x 100 x 100 (state, particle, time)
dim(res)

#> [1]   1 100 101

# Dropping the first dimension and plotting, with the mean in red
# and the expectation in blue:
xy <- t(res[1, , , drop = TRUE])
matplot(steps, xy, type = "l", lty = 1, col = "#00000033",
        xlab = "Step", ylab = "Value")

abline(h = 0, lty = 2, col = "blue")

lines(steps, rowMeans(xy), col = "red", lwd = 2)

Contents
